texto: The brain is an incredible multitasker. At the same time that it’s piercing itself with superheated needles of anguish, it’s ruthlessly making plans, contingencies, plotting out a future, giving zero fucks whether it’ll ever see it. On the day I die, it’ll be calculating what to have for dinner as it bombards itself with pain signals from my amputated legs or my clocked-out heart.

texto: I am not the heroine of this story.And I'm not trying to be cute. It's the truth. I'm diagnosed borderline and seriously fucked-up. I hold grudges. texto: Girls love each other like animals. There is something ferocious and unself-conscious about it. We don't guard ourselves like we do with boys. We love with claws and teeth and the blood is just proof of how much. It's feral.And it's relentless.

texto: Who fixes broken people? Is it only other broken people, ones who've already been ruined? And do we need to be fixed? It was the messiness and hurt in our pasts that drove us, and that same hurt connected us at a subdermal level, the kind of scars written so deeply in your cells that you can't even see them anymore, only recognize them in someone else.

texto: Kintsugi is a pottery technique. When something breaks, like a vase, they glue it back together with melted gold. Instead of making the cracks invisible, they make them beautiful. To celebrate the history of the object. What it's been through. And I was just... Thinking of us like that. My heart full of gold veins, instead of cracks.

seo.descriptionSeo: Metáfora atribuída a Leah Raeder: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Kintsugi is a pottery technique. W…

frase_original: Kintsugi is a pottery technique. When something breaks, like a vase, they glue it back together with melted gold. Instead of making the cracks invisible, they make them beautiful. To celebrate the history of the object. What it's been through. And I was just... Thinking of us like that. My heart full of gold veins, instead of cracks.

texto: Sometimes all you know about where you're going is that it's away from where you are.
explicacao: Citação de Leah Raeder sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Sometimes all you know about where you're going is that it's away from where you are.” articula tempo, escape, release em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como release.
